L1.area: Recipe execution pipeline and result display logic

L2.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where dataset table field values are rendered as HTML without proper sanitization. When a recipe processes an untrusted dataset, malicious content in table cells can execute arbitrary JavaScript code, leading to client-side remote code execution in notebook environments.

L3.area: Recipe step card rendering and HTML table display components
L3.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in the recipe card display system where dataset table cell values and step failure tracebacks are rendered as HTML without proper escaping. When a recipe processing an untrusted dataset is run in a Jupyter Notebook, malicious content embedded in data fields can execute arbitrary JavaScript, leading to client-side remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The render_table function in the recipe cards module renders dataset table cell values as HTML without escaping, and the FailedStepCard injects failure tracebacks via f-string bypassing Jinja2 escaping, allowing attacker-controlled data to execute arbitrary JavaScript in notebook environments.
